verses:
śata⋅sajjana⋅vandita pāda⋅yugaṁ,
juga⋅dharma pracāraka⋅dhurya⋅janaṁ
janatā⋅su⋅subhāṣaṇa⋅śakti⋅dharaṁ,
praṇamāmi ca mādhava⋅deva⋅padam

atidīrgha manohara gaura⋅tanuṁ,
mṛdu⋅manda⋅suhāsya⋅yutāsya⋅dharaṁ
uru⋅lambita⋅hasta⋅surūpa⋅yutaṁ,
praṇamāmi ca mādhava⋅deva⋅padam

śiśu⋅kāla⋅supāṭhya⋅suyatna⋅paraṁ,
jananī⋅savidhe⋅śruta⋅śāstra⋅mataṁ
paramārtha⋅kṛte parihīna⋅gṛhaṁ,
praṇamāmi ca mādhava⋅deva⋅padam

prabhupāda⋅pade‛rpita deha⋅matiṁ,
guru⋅kārya⋅kṛte jati⋅veśa⋅dharaṁ
praṇateṣu sadāhita⋅kāri⋅varaṁ,
praṇamāmi ca mādhava⋅deva⋅padam

prabhupāda⋅manomata kārya⋅rataṁ,
susamādṛta⋅bhaktivinoda⋅padaṁ
raghu⋅rūpa⋅sanātana⋅labdha pathaṁ,
praṇamāmi ca mādhava⋅deva⋅padam

taru⋅dhikkṛta⋅mārjana⋅śakti⋅dharaṁ,
laghu⋅sevana⋅mātraka⋅hṛṣṭa⋅hṛdaṁ
hari⋅kīrtana⋅santata⋅datta⋅matiṁ,
praṇamāmi ca mādhava⋅deva⋅padam

maṭha⋅mandira⋅nirmati kīrti⋅dharaṁ,
guru⋅gaura⋅kathāsu ca nitya⋅rataṁ
svayamācaraṇe para⋅dhairya⋅paraṁ,
praṇamāmi ca mādhava⋅deva⋅padam

karuṇārdrahṛdāhṛta viṣṇu⋅janaṁ,
jana⋅nandita vandita kṛtya⋅kulaṁ
nija⋅deśa⋅videśa suvandya⋅padaṁ,
praṇamāmi ca mādhava⋅deva⋅padam

guru⋅paŋkti⋅surakṣaṇa⋅yatna⋅paraṁ,
guru⋅sodara⋅gaurava⋅dāna⋅rataṁ
anurakta⋅susevaka⋅vākya⋅dharaṁ,
praṇamāmi ca mādhava⋅deva⋅padam

bhagavad⋅bhajanehyanurāga⋅paraṁ,
vrata⋅pālana⋅karma⋅sudārḍhya⋅yutaṁ
prabhupāda⋅padoddhṛta⋅kāri⋅janaṁ,
praṇamāmi ca mādhava⋅deva⋅padam

kṛpayā kṣamatāmaparādhi⋅janaṁ,
kaluṣāyuta⋅sakta⋅sudīna⋅naraṁ
supathe paricālaya sarva⋅dinaṁ,
praṇamāmi ca mādhava⋅deva⋅padam
en_translation:
I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Bhakti Dayita Mādhava Gosvāmī Mahārāja. His lotus feet are worshipped by hundreds of saintly persons. He is the foremost preacher of the religious practice for this age, and he possesses the power of eloquent speech.

I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Mādhava Mahārāja. His body is very tall, enchanting, and has a golden complexion. His face always holds a gentle and sweet smile, and his beautiful hands extend to his knees.

I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Mādhava Mahārāja. He was very diligent in his studies during childhood and heard the essence of the scriptures from his mother. He renounced his home for the sake of the highest spiritual truth.

I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Mādhava Mahārāja. His body and mind are dedicated to the lotus feet of Śrīla Prabhupāda. He assumed the dress of a sannyāsī for his Guru's mission and is the best benefactor to those who surrender.

I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Mādhava Mahārāja. He is engaged in the activities dear to Śrīla Prabhupāda's heart. He greatly respects the path of Śrīla Bhaktivinoda Ṭhākura and follows the path shown by the Gosvāmīs—Rūpa, Sanātana, and Raghunātha.

I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Mādhava Mahārāja. He has the power to cleanse the heart more than any other means. His heart is gladdened by even a small service, and his mind is constantly engaged in the congregational chanting of Lord Hari's names.

I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Mādhava Mahārāja. He established great fame by building temples and was always absorbed in topics concerning the Guru and Lord Gaura. He is supremely patient in his own conduct.

I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Mādhava Mahārāja. His compassionate heart attracts all the devotees. All his activities are worshipped by the people, and his feet are venerable in his own country and abroad.

I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Mādhava Mahārāja. He is diligent in protecting the lineage of spiritual masters and is always engaged in bestowing honor upon the Guru's associates. He values the words of his devoted servants.

I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Mādhava Mahārāja. He is intensely attached to the devotional service of the Supreme Lord and is firm in following his vows. He delivers many souls at the lotus feet of Śrīla Prabhupāda.

I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Śrīla Mādhava Mahārāja. By his mercy, he forgives all offenders. He always guides the most fallen and sinful persons onto the right path.
